mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts amplifies aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annually during monetary tightening cycles indicates resilient supply-side capacity that supports equity valuations despite higher interest rates, yielding positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The positive return signal reverses to negative during tightening periods or fails to achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth rates reflect expanding economic output that directly increases corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across multiple expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3% annually forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, which increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of high-inflation months or the negative predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates creates a tight labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of occurrences or interaction term coefficients become statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during post-shock recovery phases indicates lingering supply constraints that suppress corporate output and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative return predictions fail to materialize during recovery phases or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.01 in regime-specific walk-forward tests.
